perturbation.original_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "PRIVATE DATA" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" with "PRIVATE DATA" within the definition section. Later sections refer to "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION," leading to uncertainty of whether "PRIVATE DATA" and "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" have the same meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'. The changed text replaces this with 'PRIVATE DATA' in the definition. The contradicted text refers exclusively to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', creating a contradiction because it's no longer clear if 'PRIVATE DATA' is the same as 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' and thus subject to the confidentiality obligations in the contradicted text. This creates uncertainty regarding which term should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition. The change introduces a direct conflict by rendering the continued use of the term 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: No information will be regarded as CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION if the Party to which it is disclosed can show by competent proof that such information (a) was at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became, through no fault of the receiving Party, known to the general public through publication or otherwise; or (b) was, subsequent to disclosure to a Party, lawfully and independently received by that Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it without restriction.
perturbation.changed_text: No information will be regarded as PROTECTED MATERIAL if the Party to which it is disclosed can show by competent proof that such information (a) was at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became, through no fault of the receiving Party, known to the general public through publication or otherwise; or (b) was, subsequent to disclosure to a Party, lawfully and independently received by that Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" with "PROTECTED MATERIAL" in the exception criteria, while the definition of confidential information remains valid creates confusion. Does this mean information previously classified as confidential is no longer subject to the same terms?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any Party, at its discretion, may disclose to another Party any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that the disclosing Party, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Party to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA. Any Party may also cause such disclosures to be made to the other Party on behalf of the disclosing Party by third parties who are Under obligations of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; such disclosures from third parties shall be deemed to be disclosures by the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" with "PROTECTED MATERIAL" in the exception criteria. The contradicted text refers to "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION." Since the contract now uses two potentially distinct terms ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" and "PROTECTED MATERIAL") without defining the relationship between them, it creates ambiguity about which information is subject to the disclosure terms in the contradicted text. The explanation states that this replacement creates confusion, suggesting information previously classified as confidential is no longer subject to the same terms. Therefore, it creates a contradiction because the scope of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', as referenced in the contradicted text, is now unclear due to the introduction of the new term in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Specific aspects or details of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be deemed to be within the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is embraced by general disclosures in the public domain or in the possession of a Party. In addition, any combination of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be considered in the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because individual elements thereof are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party unless the combination and its principles are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Specific aspects or details of COMPANY SECRETS shall not be deemed to be within the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is embraced by general disclosures in the public domain or in the possession of a Party. In addition, any combination of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be considered in the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because individual elements thereof are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party unless the combination and its principles are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party.
perturbation.explanation: Switching from "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" to "COMPANY SECRETS" but keeping "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" later in the same paragraph creates ambiguity. The definition in section 1 defines "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" not "COMPANY SECRETS". Are they the same? This contradiction impacts the understanding of what is considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Specific aspects or details of COMPANY SECRETS shall not be deemed to be within the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is embraced by general disclosures in the public domain or in the possession of a Party. In addition, any combination of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be considered in the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because individual elements thereof are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party unless the combination and its principles are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'COMPANY SECRETS' while retaining 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' in the same paragraph. Since 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' is defined in Section 1, and 'COMPANY SECRETS' is not, this creates ambiguity regarding which information is protected. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which highlights the in-text contradiction due to the misaligned terminology, leading to uncertainty about what constitutes confidential information. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Any Party, at its discretion, may disclose to another Party any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that the disclosing Party, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Party to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Party, at its discretion, may disclose to another Party any PROPRIETARY DATA that the disclosing Party, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Party to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" with "PROPRIETARY DATA" impacts the discretionary disclosure clause. If "PROPRIETARY DATA" isn't equivalent to "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION," the disclosure terms become conflicting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', which is explicitly defined and subsequently governed by specific obligations in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' with 'PROPRIETARY DATA'. If 'PROPRIETARY DATA' is not explicitly defined as equivalent to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' within the document, the obligations outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' (which pertain specifically to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION') no longer clearly apply to the disclosed 'PROPRIETARY DATA'. This creates a contradiction because the rules governing confidential information are now unclear when proprietary data is disclosed, fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: All COMPANY ASSETS will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" to "COMPANY ASSETS" in the ownership clause, but retaining "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" for return obligations, introduces confusion about which data needs to be returned upon request, if not all assets are "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" or not all "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" are the assets mentioned
perturbation.contradicted_text: All COMPANY ASSETS will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' with 'COMPANY ASSETS' in the property ownership clause but retains 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' in the return obligation. This creates a contradiction because the return obligation refers to 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' while the ownership clause now refers to 'COMPANY ASSETS'. This introduces ambiguity about whether all company assets are confidential information, and whether the receiving party must return all company assets or only confidential information. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, thus there is an inconsistency as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
